---
title: "Table Interchange"
subtitle: "`as_feature_table()`, `as_event_table()`, `as_object_table()`, `merge_feature_tables()`."
sidebar: concepts
---

## What it is

Table interchange turns typed feature objects into pandas tables for inspection, modeling, and joins. The object remains the in-memory contract; the table is the rectangular exchange form.

## Invariants

- `as_feature_table()` emits one row per time-by-feature value.
- `as_event_table()` emits one row per event.
- `as_object_table()` normalizes event or track objects to object-centric columns such as `object_id`, `object_type`, `onset_s`, and geometry fields.
- `as_temporal_table()` dispatches by object type.
- `merge_feature_tables(format="wide")` accepts only `FeatureSeries` outputs.
- Temporal tables include `time_reference`; bounded rows include onset and offset.

## What it refuses

- Missing pandas when table output is requested.
- Wide merges that include `EventSeries` or `TrackSeries` outputs.
- Event or track extra columns whose lengths do not match the object row count.
- Cross-clock wide merges without a temporal context, target clock, and join policy.

## What it doesn't do

Table helpers do not resample features or invent timeline mappings. Use
`ExtractFeaturesResult.align_to()` first when you need to record how source rows
map to word, event, frame, or custom timelines. For heterogeneous native grids,
keep a `FeatureBundle` and defer rectangularization to the downstream analysis.

## Canonical event columns

```{python}
from natural_features.core.interchange import OBJECT_CANONICAL_COLUMNS

OBJECT_CANONICAL_COLUMNS[:10]
```

## See also

[Align features to a timeline](../cookbook/timeline-alignment.qmd) · [Export events to a table](../cookbook/event-table-export.qmd) · [Merge feature tables](../cookbook/feature-table-merge.qmd)
